questions.text: What is the recurring refrain that follows the story of every Prophet mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not believers. And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

questions.text: How did Allah comfort the Prophet (SAW) regarding his grief over the disbelievers?
questions.answer: He asked rhetorically if the Prophet would kill himself with grief because they do not believe, and reminded him that if He willed, He could send a sign from heaven that would force their necks to bow.

questions.text: What was Pharaoh's personal argument against Musa (AS) when he first arrived?
questions.answer: He reminded Musa that they had raised him as a child among them and accused him of being ungrateful for the 'favor' they did for him.

questions.text: What was the 'miracle of the staff' as described in this Surah?
questions.answer: It swallowed up the falsehood (illusions) that the sorcerers produced.

questions.text: What did the sorcerers say to Pharaoh after they were threatened with crucifixion?
questions.answer: They said 'No harm (Lā dayr). Indeed, to our Lord we will return'.

questions.text: Describe Ibrahim's (AS) description of his Lord in his debate with his people.
questions.answer: He described Allah as the One who created him, guides him, feeds him, gives him drink, heals him when sick, and will cause him to die then bring him to life.

questions.text: What was Ibrahim's (AS) prayer regarding his 'mention' in the future?
questions.answer: He asked Allah to 'grant me a reputation of honor among later generations'.

questions.text: Who will be saved on the Day of Judgment according to Ibrahim's (AS) prayer?
questions.answer: Only those who come to Allah with a 'Qalbun Salim' (a sound/pure heart).

questions.text: What was the specific message of Prophet Nuh to his people in this Surah?
questions.answer: To fear Allah and obey him; he asked for no reward as his reward was only with the Lord of the worlds.

questions.text: What did the people of 'Ad take pride in building (Verse 128)?
questions.answer: Monuments/signs on every high place for amusement and strongholds as if they would live forever.

questions.text: What was the sin of the people of Salih regarding the She-Camel?
questions.answer: They hamstrung (killed) her after being warned not to touch her with harm.

questions.text: How did the people of Lut respond to his call to morality?
questions.answer: They threatened to expel him from the city because he and his followers were 'people who keep themselves pure'.

questions.text: What was the specific message of Prophet Nuh to his people in this Surah?
questions.answer: To fear Allah and obey him; he asked for no reward as his reward was only with the Lord of the worlds.

questions.text: What is the Quranic response to the claim that Devils (Shayatin) brought down the Quran?
questions.answer: It is not suited for them, they are not able to do it, and they are removed from even hearing it.

questions.text: What instruction did Allah give the Prophet (SAW) regarding his relatives in Verse 214?
questions.answer: And warn, [O Muhammad], your closest kindred.

questions.text: How should the Prophet treat the believers who follow him?
questions.answer: He should lower his wing (be kind and humble) to them.

questions.text: Why is the Surah named 'The Poets'?
questions.answer: Because it concludes by distinguishing the Prophet from poets, noting that poets are followed by the deviant and wander in every valley of speech.

questions.text: Which poets are exempted from the general criticism at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: Those who believe, do righteous deeds, remember Allah much, and defend themselves after they were wronged.

questions.text: What happened to the people of Nuh when they rejected him?
questions.answer: They were drowned in the flood.

questions.text: What is the 'clear proof' Musa (AS) brought to Pharaoh?
questions.answer: The miracles of the staff and his shining white hand.
